layman_headline: Market is recovering from deep fear but these alt candidates are too risky to touch right now.
layman_explanation: The crypto market has been in a fear zone for the past week (averaging 21 out of 100) but is slowly improving to 27 today. Bitcoin bounced 8.5% this week, and some altcoins rallied even harder — ZEC up 23%, ADA up 22%, ETH up 13%. However, the swing trade candidates available are either crashing tokens with no clear bottom (LAB down 62% today, GRASS down 20%), already-extended runners (LDO up 31%, MON up 34%), or coins with bearish chart structures and terrible risk/reward ratios (FET). None of these present a clean swing entry with defined risk.
layman_action: Stay in cash and wait. The market is recovering but hasn't confirmed a trend reversal yet. A Fed document due Wednesday could be the real catalyst. Watch for a pullback in the stronger performers (ETH, SOL, ZEC) to create better entry points.
expert_reasoning: The Fear & Greed Index at 27 (improving from weekly avg of 21) suggests we're exiting extreme fear, which historically precedes a relief rally — but we're not yet in a confirmed recovery. BTC dominance at 56.1% is high, meaning capital is flowing to BTC over alts, making alt swing trades riskier. Among candidates: LAB's -62% daily move screams distressed asset with unknown catalysts — catching knives is not swing trading. LDO/MON are up 30%+ in 7 days with no pullback entry. FET's chart analysis shows bearish structure below all MAs with resistance at $0.1872 and support at $0.10 — a risk/reward of 0.10x is essentially untradeable. The responsible call is to wait for either a pullback in strong performers or a confirmed technical setup with proper risk/reward.
risk_factors: Fed document on Wednesday could trigger volatility in either direction, invalidating any position taken now
Fear & Greed still in fear territory — market hasn't confirmed a bottom and could retest lows
High BTC dominance (56.1%) means alt positions face additional headwind from capital rotation to BTC
watch_list: Fed document release on Wednesday — could be the decisive catalyst for BTC's next move
ETH and SOL for pullback entries if they retrace 5-8% from current levels while maintaining weekly uptrend
ZEC continuation — up 23% weekly with strong volume ($540M); a pullback to $0.95-1.00 area could offer a clean swing entry
market_context: Crypto is in early recovery mode after a week of extreme fear (avg 21). BTC bounced 8.5% but remains below key psychological levels. A Fed document due Wednesday is the next major catalyst that will determine if this bounce has legs.
context_banner: Market recovering from extreme fear with BTC up 8.5% weekly, but available swing candidates lack clean setups — patience is the best trade right now.

key_signals.description: Fear & Greed at 27, improving from weekly avg of 21 — exiting extreme fear but not yet confirming recovery, aligning with validated rule that extreme fear can precede further drops before a real bounce.
Bitcoin rally depends on a Fed document coming Wednesday — this creates binary event risk that makes entering new positions ahead of it inadvisable.
FET chart shows bearish structure below all moving averages with a dismal 0.10x risk/reward ratio — the only candidate with chart data and it's clearly untradeable.
ZEC surged 23% on $540M volume and is the strongest performer in top 20 — worth monitoring for a pullback entry but not chasing at current levels.
BTC dominance at 56.1% signals capital rotation away from alts, adding structural headwind to any altcoin swing trade attempt.
